summary: Fighter decompilation PR focused on Kirby copy-ability cleanup/hat loading and common fighter death/rebirth behavior. It replaced placeholder comments with implementations for Kirby SpecialN handlers, DeadUpFall physics, and a RebirthWait transition helper, plus exposed one callback prototype and refined ftCommon motion-vars layout for DeadUp data. The PR was closed without merge; the bot reported +684 matched bytes, with 3 newly matched functions and 2 still-imperfect improvements.
what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 in ftKb_Init.c to load Kirby's Yoshi copy-ability hat resources when Kirby first copies Yoshi Neutral Special, including hat allocation via HSD_ObjAlloc, setup calls through ftKb_SpecialN_800EF040/800EF0E4/800EF35C/800EF438, ftParts_8007487C, ftAnim_80070200, and ftCo_8009D81C.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC as a switch over fp->fv.kb.hat.kind that dispatches stock-loss cleanup to copied-character-specific handlers for Popo, Peach, Fox/Falco, Link/Young Link, Mewtwo, Ness, Samus, Sheik, Yoshi, Donkey, and Game & Watch.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C as a very similar respawn/revival-platform cleanup dispatcher, with the notable Mewtwo case calling ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107130 instead of ftKb_SpecialNMt_8010709C.
Expanded ftCommon_MotionVars.unk_deadup in ftCommon/types.h by replacing an opaque pad from 0x48 to 0x68 with x4C float, x50 Vec3, and x5C Vec3 fields before x68.

Implemented ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys in ft_0D31.c to handle DeadUpFall physics cases: interpolate between common-data Vec3 points using lbVector_Lerp for state 1, and apply fall/gravity, velocity accumulation, position accumulation, and vector clearing for state 3.

Implemented ftCo_800D5600 in ft_0D31.c to reset collision/position, zero vertical self velocity, set common motion var x0 from p_ftCommonData->x5D4, change to ftCo_MS_RebirthWait with keep/skip motion flags, set fighter state bits, choose an accessory callback based on fp->x221F_b4, and refresh collision again when smash_attrs.x2135 is set.
Added includes needed by the new ft_0D31.c implementations: ftanim.h, lbvector.h, and mpcoll.h.
Added fn_800D54A4 prototype to ft_0D31.h so the rebirth transition code can assign it as an accessory callback.
smart_moves: The Kirby cleanup functions model copied-character behavior as a switch on FighterKind, grouping shared handlers where appropriate, such as Fox/Falco and Link/CLINK, which mirrors the likely original dispatch structure and keeps copy-ability resource cleanup centralized.

The DeadUpFall match exposed real motion-variable fields in ftCommon_MotionVars instead of continuing to index into a padding blob, making later star-KO/death physics work easier and less error-prone.

The ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys implementation uses p_ftCommonData byte offsets for constants while giving fighter-local working vectors typed names, a pragmatic balance when common-data structs are not fully recovered.

The Yoshi hat loader preserves matching constraints with #pragma dont_inline, a large temporary stack buffer, and PAD_STACK, showing that local variable layout and inlining control were important for this function.

The rebirth transition implementation keeps the motion-state flag expression explicit, documenting that graphics, accessory, collision-animation, and nametag visibility behavior must be preserved/skipped during the transition.
decomp_lessons: When a function writes into previously padded motion-vars storage, recover the struct fields in the shared type definition at the same time as the function match; this prevents future code from relying on raw padding offsets.
Switch dispatch over FighterKind is a useful pattern for Kirby copy-ability cleanup functions, especially where multiple fighters share the same copied-special resource behavior.

Near-duplicate lifecycle functions can still differ in one case handler; here the stock-loss and respawn cleanup dispatchers are almost identical, but Mewtwo calls different functions in ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC versus ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C.
For hard-to-match fighter init/resource functions, matching may require explicit no-inline pragmas, dummy stack buffers, and PAD_STACK to recreate the original stack frame.

Common-data offsets can be used as an interim tactic when the containing data structure is not fully named, but typed local fields such as Vec3 motion vars should be recovered wherever evidence exists.
A PR title/body may overstate match status: the body says 5 matched functions, while the bot reported only 3 new 100% matches and 2 improvements that remained at 99.97% and 81.20%.
naming_conventions: Kirby copy-special functions use the ftKb_SpecialN_* prefix with address suffixes for unnamed or partially named functions.

Character-specific Kirby copied Neutral Special helpers use prefixes like ftKb_SpecialNIc, ftKb_SpecialNPe, ftKb_SpecialNFx, ftKb_SpecialNLk, ftKb_SpecialNMt, ftKb_SpecialNNs, ftKb_SpecialNSs, ftKb_SpecialNSk, ftKb_SpecialNYs, ftKb_SpecialNPr, and ftKb_SpecialNGw.
Common fighter state functions use ftCo_* names, such as ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys and ftCo_RebirthWait_Anim.
Unidentified callbacks retain fn_address naming, as with fn_800D54A4 and fn_800D55B4.
Motion-vars fields in the recovered unk_deadup struct were named by offset, such as x4C, x50, x5C, and x68, preserving layout evidence without inventing semantic names.

FighterKind enum constants appear as FTKIND_* values, including FTKIND_POPO, FTKIND_PEACH, FTKIND_FOX, FTKIND_FALCO, FTKIND_LINK, FTKIND_CLINK, FTKIND_MEWTWO, FTKIND_NESS, FTKIND_SAMUS, FTKIND_SEAK, FTKIND_YOSHI, FTKIND_DONKEY, and FTKIND_GAMEWATCH.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 to constrain compiler behavior.
Inserted a local u8 sp14[0x90] and PAD_STACK(8) in ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 to shape the stack frame for matching.

Kept some accesses to p_ftCommonData as byte offsets in ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys, including offsets around 0x520, 0x18, 0x24, 0x34, and 0x38, likely because the full common-data substructure was not named.
Used lbVector_Lerp and lbVector_Add with recovered Vec3 fields to express vector operations while matching the original code shape.
Grouped switch cases with fallthrough where two FighterKind values shared a handler, reducing duplicated code and matching likely compiler switch generation.

Bot evidence showed complete matches for ftCo_800D5600, ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C, while ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys and ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 were improvements but not fully matched.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 61.12%, +0.02%, +684 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 3 new 100% matches: ftCo_800D5600 (+236 bytes), ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC (+224 bytes), and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C (+224 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 2 unmatched-item improvements: ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys reached 99.97% (+235 bytes), and ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 reached 81.20% (+175 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that fully match ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 after this 81.20% attempt.
Look for later PRs that resolve the remaining ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys 99.97% mismatch.
Search for additional recovery of p_ftCommonData fields around offsets 0x520, 0x538, 0x544, 0x554, and 0x558.

Check whether PR #2366 was superseded by another fighter decompilation PR because this one was closed unmerged.
